sourceNote: TCGplayer's published pull-rate study for Prismatic Evolutions, based on 1,200 booster packs opened (their stated sample; smaller than their usual because this is a special set). Headline figures: Special Illustration Rare 1 in 45 packs — roughly twice the SIR rate of the preceding four sets — and ACE SPEC about 1 in 21. Per-tier odds here follow that study as republished by ThePriceDex (Hyper Rare 1 in 178.6, SIR 1 in 45.0, Ultra Rare 1 in 13.4, ACE SPEC 1 in 21.4, Double Rare 1 in 6.1), which match TCGplayer's headline numbers where both state them. Rated medium rather than high deliberately: the primary article is client-side rendered and was verified through two independent republications rather than read directly. Structure note, verified against the live catalog: this set has NO Illustration Rare tier (32 SIRs, 0 IRs, 180 cards). Every card also exists as a Poke Ball or Master Ball reverse pattern (community figures ~1 in 3 and ~1 in 20 packs respectively); those are printing variants of existing cards rather than separate tiers, and are not yet modelled — EV here slightly UNDERSTATES pack value as a result. God packs exist with no reliable published odds (community aggregates put them anywhere from 1 in 1,500 to 1 in 4,000); they are likewise unmodelled.

alternateEstimates.note: A documented 200-pack community opening (the source itself concedes supply shortages prevented a statistically significant sample) reports SIR at roughly 1 in 53 packs, ACE SPEC 1 in 29, and Hyper Rare 1 in 152 — SIR ~15% rarer than TCGplayer's 1 in 45. At n=200 the disagreement is well within clustering error.
